title: The Supreme Court Judges (Remuneration and Privileges) Ordinance, 1978
title_en: The Supreme Court Judges (Remuneration and Privileges) Ordinance, 1978
description: An Ordinance to provide for the remuneration and privileges of the Judges of the Supreme Court. WHEREAS it is expedient to provide for the remuneration and privileges of the Judges of the Supreme Court; NOW, THEREFORE, in pursuance of the Proclamations of the 20th August, 1975, and the 8th November, 1975, and in exercise of all powers enabling him in that behalf, the President is pleased to make and promulgate the...

provisions.title: 1. Short title
provisions.content: 1. This Ordinance may be called the Supreme Court Judges (Remuneration and Privileges) Ordinance, 1978 .

provisions.title: 2. Definition
provisions.content: 2. In this Ordinance, unless there is anything repugnant in the subject or context, “Judge” means a Judge of a Division of the Supreme Court, and includes the Chief Justice and an Additional Judge of a Division of that Court.

provisions.title: 3. Salary
provisions.content:
3. [(1) There shall be paid to the Judges a salary at the following rates, namely:-
(i) The Chief Justice - Taka 1,10,000 Per mensem;
(ii) A Judge of the Appellate - Taka 1,05,000 Per mensem;
Division
(iii) A Judge of the High Court - Taka 95,000 Per mensem.]
Division
(2) No income-tax shall be payable in respect of [salary and allowances payable to a Judge under this Ordinance].

provisions.title: 4. [Omitted]
provisions.content: 3A. [Omitted by section 3 of the Supreme Court Judges (Remuneration and Privileges) (Amendment) Ordinance, 1985 (Ordinance No. XLV of 1985).]

provisions.title: 5. [Omitted]
provisions.content: 3B.[Omitted by section 3 of the Supreme Court Judges (Remuneration and Privileges) (Amendment) Act, 1992 (Act No. XIII of 1992).]

provisions.title: 6. [Omitted]
provisions.content: 3C.[Omitted by section 3 of the Supreme Court Judges (Remuneration and Privileges) (Amendment) Act, 1992 (Act No. XIII of 1992).]

provisions.title: 8. Special Allowance
provisions.content: [3DD. There shall be paid to a judge a special allowance at the rate of 50% of his salary per mensem.]

provisions.title: 9. Recreation allowance.
provisions.content:
[3E. (1) There shall be paid to a judge, a recreation allowance equal to one month's pay of his salary once in every three years for the purpose of taking leave for rest and recreation.
(2) The recreation allowance shall be paid in addition to the leave salary as mentioned in the section 7 of the Supreme Court Judges (Leave, Pension and Privileges) Ordinance, 1982 (Ordinance No. XX of 1982).]

provisions.title: 10. Festival allowance
provisions.content:
[3F. (1) There shall be paid to a Judge festival allowance equal to two month’s pay of his salary in every year.
(2) The allowance under sub-section (1) shall be paid following the provisions mentioned in the Finance Division’s Memo No. FM/FD(Imp)-4/FB-12/86/29, dated 3 July 1988, and other notifications issued, from time to time, in this behalf by the Government.]

provisions.title: 11. Bangla New Year allowance
provisions.content:
[3G. (1) There shall be paid to a Judge a Bangla New Year allowance once in every year at the rate of 20% of his salary per mensem.
(2) The allowance under sub-section (1) shall be paid following the provisions mentioned in the Finance Division’s Memo No. 07.00.0000.173.44.018.14-78, deted 14 October 2015.]

provisions.title: 12. Privileges regarding residence, etc.
13. Medical facilities
provisions.content: 5. A Judge and the members of his family shall be entitled to medical facilities admissible under the Special Medical Attendance Rules, except that he and the members of his family shall be entitled to medical treatment at the residence of the Judge; and such medical facilities shall continue to be admissible after the Judge has retired or otherwise ceased to hold office.

provisions.title: 14. Rights relating to leave, pension, etc.
provisions.content:
6. (1) In respect of leave, pension, gratuity and provident fund, a Judge shall be entitled, subject to the provisions of this Ordinance, to all the rights, privileges and allowances as were admissible to him immediately before the commencement of this Ordinance.
(2) [Omitted by section 4 of the Supreme Court Judges (Remuneration and Privileges) (Amendment) Act, 2005 (Act No. XXV of 2005).]

provisions.title: 15. Option of Judges
provisions.content: 7. This Ordinance shall not apply to a Judge who, immediately before the commencement of this Ordinance, was holding office as a Judge of the [High Court Division] or [Appellate Division of the Supreme Court] and who, within a period of three months from such commencement, exercises in writing communicated to the Accountant General of Bangladesh his option to be governed by the provisions of law which, immediately before such commencement, were applicable to him.

provisions.title: 16. Repeal
provisions.content: 8. The Supreme Court and the High Court Judges (Remuneration and Privileges) Ordinance, 1976 (LXV of 1976) is hereby repealed.

provisions.title: 17. Retrospective effect
provisions.content: 9. The provisions of this Ordinance shall be deemed to have taken effect on the 1st day of December, 1977.

definitions.definition: a Judge of a Division of the Supreme Court, and includes the Chief Justice and an Additional Judge of a Division of that Court.
